sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Small objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] < 200:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")

# Check character position
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] == 40:
        print(f"\nCharacter: boundary={n['boundary'][:6]}")
        break

# Check charcoal
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c':
        print(f"Charcoal: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:6]}")

# Check yellow L-shape
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] > 40:
        print(f"Yellow: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:6]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Now I see what happened:
- The character (id=6, black, 40px) is now at cols 36-44 in the right panel
- The charcoal L-shape (id=5, 45px) is back at its original position in the left panel (cols 18-26)
- The yellow L-shape (id=12, 45px) is still at cols 51-59 in the right panel
- The divider (id=1, sky blue, 189px) is back to its original full height
So it seems like when the character crossed the divider, something happened that reset the charcoal object to its original position, and the character continued moving into the right panel. The character is now in the right panel, between the charcoal L-shape (which is now on the left) and the yellow L-shape.
The character is at rows 15-23, cols 36-44, and the yellow L-shape is at rows 45-53, cols 51-59. So the character needs to move DOWN and RIGHT to reach the yellow L-shape.
Let me try moving DOWN to see if the character can reach the yellow object.
